rows.definition: The deployable, declarative authorization layer that defines exactly which tools an agent may call by name, loaded as a JSON manifest at runtime.
rows.distinction: The abstract authorization INTENT - NOT the policy manifest (its on-disk JSON) and NOT the adjudicator (the enforcer that consults it).

rows.definition: The versioned JSON structure an operator edits to configure the capability floor, mapping 1:1 to adjudicator.Policy with deny reasons validated against the closed vocabulary.
rows.distinction: The on-disk JSON REPRESENTATION - NOT the capability floor (the abstract intent) and NOT Policy (the compiled in-memory form).

rows.definition: The in-memory decision table compiled from a manifest, holding allow-lists, deny maps, arg predicates, posture, and secret patterns the adjudicator consults.
rows.distinction: The COMPILED in-memory table - NOT the manifest (the on-disk JSON) and NOT the adjudicator (the enforcer that reads it).
gaps: the compiled-Policy vs manifest distinction is defined-only

rows.definition: The in-process DOS reference monitor: the rung that folds a decision chain to prove a tool call allowed, denied, or deferred under the loaded policy.
rows.distinction: The ENFORCER - NOT policy (the rule table it consults) and NOT a verdict (the decision it returns).

rows.definition: The discriminated-union decision an adjudicator returns, keyed by kind (Allow, Deny, Defer, Transform, Quarantine, RequireWitness) with typed payloads.
rows.distinction: The full DECISION (kind + payload + reason) - NOT a reason code (only the WHY of a deny) and NOT session.Verdict (the per-turn boundary gate).

rows.definition: The closed, additive vocabulary of refusal reasons (DEFAULT_DENY, POLICY_BLOCK, SELF_MODIFY, ...) that every deny verdict cites, never free text.
rows.distinction: The WHY of a deny (closed vocabulary) - NOT the verdict (the WHAT, which embeds the reason) and NOT posture (the default-deny behavior).

rows.definition: The policy's default behavior after all provable refusal checks pass: PostureFailClosed (deny everything not allowed) or PostureAdmitAndLog (admit low-risk reads with forensic metadata).
rows.distinction: The CALL-admit default-deny knob - NOT secret posture (the RESULT-bearing-a-credential knob) and NOT a reason code.

rows.definition: The cheapest-first well-formedness rung ladder that catches malformed / unsafe calls before they fire, registered ahead of the authoritative monitor.
rows.distinction: STATIC well-formedness checks (parse, schema) - NOT the adjudicator (authorization enforcement) and NOT a single rung within it.
gaps: preflight ladder vs adjudicator vs rung not yet fully in the glossary
